revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster | native_name =作山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukuriyama Kofungun (Yosano) gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|30|21.82|N|135|06|16.96|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Yosano, Kyoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|30|21.82|N|135|06|16.96|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster'''|作山古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Yosano neighborhood of the town of [[Yosano, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The group consists of five tumuli which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191122 |title=作山古墳群 |trans-title= Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster is located at the base of the [[Tango Peninsula]] in northern Kyoto Prefecture. It consists of a total of five tumuli: one {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, two {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofuns and two {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style square Kofuns. Although of different shapes, all are small to medium-sized tumuli and are roughly contemporary, dating from the late [[Kofun period]], or the first half of the 5th century. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1929 and 1989, during which time a large quantity of cylindrical and figurative (house shaped, equipment shaped) ''[[haniwa]]'' were discovered. In the keyhole-shaped tumulus, a granite sarcophagus was found in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the bones of an adult male. [[Grave goods]] included [[bronze mirror]]s, beads and other jewelry, iron swords, iron spearheads, and iron farming implements such as sickles. A total of 26 burials were discovered in and around the remaining tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' were also excavated.
'''Kofun No.1''' - This is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 28 meters and height of four meters, in two tiers. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and ''haniwa''. The sarcophagus was made of granite and was located in a burial chamber with a vestibule. Grave goods included one bronze mirror, two stone swords, 12 iron swords, four spearheads and two iron axes and two iron sickles, as well as 500 glass beads. In additional 15 subordinate burials were found at the foot of the mound.
'''Kofun No.2''' - This is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 26 meters and height of 3.5 meters. ''Haniwa'' have been found on the surface, but no ''fukiishi''. The details of the burial chamber are unclear, as the tumulus has not been excavated. In addition, 10 subordinate burials are found on the west side of the mound.
'''Kofun No.3''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 17 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. ''Haniwa'' have been found on the surface, but no ''fukiishi''. The details of the burial chamber are unclear; however, excavations have found large pot which originated from the western end of the [[Seto Inland Sea]].
'''Kofun No.4''' - This is a small [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] with a total length of 29 meters.Both ''Haniwa'' and ''fukiishi'' been found on the surface. The details of the burial chamber are unclear.
'''Kofun No.5''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 13 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. The burial chamber contained a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin with a length of 6.4 meters.Numerous grave goods were found.
Currently, the site, together with the [[Ebisuyama Kofun]], is open to the public as the Yosano Municipal Kofun Park after the restoration.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-1, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.1 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-2, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.2 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-3, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.3 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-4, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.4 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-5, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.4 作山1号墳・5号墳出土 金属製品.JPG|Metal grave goods from Kofun No.1 and No.5 作山1号墳出土 合子形埴輪・丹後型円筒埴輪.JPG|Haniwa from Kofun No.1 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]]

==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsukuriyama Kofun Group}} *[https://yosano-kankou.net/kankou/kofunpark/ Yosano Tourism home page] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster | native_name =作山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukuriyama Kofungun (Yosano) gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|30|21.82|N|135|06|16.96|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Yosano, Kyoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|30|21.82|N|135|06|16.96|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster'''|作山古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Yosano neighborhood of the town of [[Yosano, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The group consists of five tumuli which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191122 |title=作山古墳群 |trans-title= Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster is located at the base of the [[Tango Peninsula]] in northern Kyoto Prefecture. It consists of a total of five tumuli: one {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, two {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofuns and two {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style square Kofuns. Although of different shapes, all are small to medium-sized tumuli and are roughly contemporary, dating from the late [[Kofun period]], or the first half of the 5th century. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1929 and 1989, during which time a large quantity of cylindrical and figurative (house shaped, equipment shaped) ''[[haniwa]]'' were discovered. In the keyhole-shaped tumulus, a granite sarcophagus was found in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the bones of an adult male. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, beads and other jewelry, iron swords, iron spearheads, and iron farming implements such as sickles. A total of 26 burials were discovered in and around the remaining tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' were also excavated.
'''Kofun No.1''' - This is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 28 meters and height of four meters, in two tiers. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and ''haniwa''. The sarcophagus was made of granite and was located in a burial chamber with a vestibule. Grave goods included one bronze mirror, two stone swords, 12 iron swords, four spearheads and two iron axes and two iron sickles, as well as 500 glass beads. In additional 15 subordinate burials were found at the foot of the mound.
'''Kofun No.2''' - This is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 26 meters and height of 3.5 meters. ''Haniwa'' have been found on the surface, but no ''fukiishi''. The details of the burial chamber are unclear, as the tumulus has not been excavated. In addition, 10 subordinate burials are found on the west side of the mound.
'''Kofun No.3''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 17 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. ''Haniwa'' have been found on the surface, but no ''fukiishi''. The details of the burial chamber are unclear; however, excavations have found large pot which originated from the western end of the [[Seto Inland Sea]].
'''Kofun No.4''' - This is a small [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] with a total length of 29 meters.Both ''Haniwa'' and ''fukiishi'' been found on the surface. The details of the burial chamber are unclear.
'''Kofun No.5''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 13 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. The burial chamber contained a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin with a length of 6.4 meters.Numerous grave goods were found.
Currently, the site, together with the [[Ebisuyama Kofun]], is open to the public as the Yosano Municipal Kofun Park after the restoration.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-1, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.1 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-2, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.2 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-3, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.3 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-4, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.4 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-5, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.4 作山1号墳・5号墳出土 金属製品.JPG|Metal grave goods from Kofun No.1 and No.5 作山1号墳出土 合子形埴輪・丹後型円筒埴輪.JPG|Haniwa from Kofun No.1 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsukuriyama Kofun Group}} *[https://yosano-kankou.net/kankou/kofunpark/ Yosano Tourism home page] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster | native_name =作山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukuriyama Kofungun (Yosano) gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|30|21.82|N|135|06|16.96|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Yosano, Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|30|21.82|N|135|06|16.96|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster'''|作山古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Yosano neighborhood of the town of [[Yosano, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The group consists of five tumuli which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191122 |title=作山古墳群 |trans-title= Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster is located at the base of the [[Tango Peninsula]] in northern Kyoto Prefecture. It consists of a total of five tumuli: one {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, two {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofuns and two {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style square Kofuns. Although of different shapes, all are small to medium-sized tumuli and are roughly contemporary, dating from the late [[Kofun period]], or the first half of the 5th century. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1929 and 1989, during which time a large quantity of cylindrical and figurative (house shaped, equipment shaped) ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were discovered. In the keyhole-shaped tumulus, a granite sarcophagus was found in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the bones of an adult male. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, beads and other jewelry, iron swords, iron spearheads, and iron farming implements such as sickles. A total of 26 burials were discovered in and around the remaining tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were also excavated.
'''Kofun No.1''' - This is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 28 meters and height of four meters, in two tiers. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and ''haniwa''. The sarcophagus was made of granite and was located in a burial chamber with a vestibule. Grave goods included one bronze mirror, two stone swords, 12 iron swords, four spearheads and two iron axes and two iron sickles, as well as 500 glass beads. In additional 15 subordinate burials were found at the foot of the mound.
'''Kofun No.2''' - This is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 26 meters and height of 3.5 meters. ''Haniwa'' have been found on the surface, but no ''fukiishi''. The details of the burial chamber are unclear, as the tumulus has not been excavated. In addition, 10 subordinate burials are found on the west side of the mound.
'''Kofun No.3''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 17 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. ''Haniwa'' have been found on the surface, but no ''fukiishi''. The details of the burial chamber are unclear; however, excavations have found large pot which originated from the western end of the [[Seto Inland Sea]].
'''Kofun No.4''' - This is a small [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] with a total length of 29 meters.Both ''Haniwa'' and ''fukiishi'' been found on the surface. The details of the burial chamber are unclear.
'''Kofun No.5''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 13 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. The burial chamber contained a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin with a length of 6.4 meters.Numerous grave goods were found.
Currently, the site, together with the [[Ebisuyama Kofun]], is open to the public as the Yosano Municipal Kofun Park after the restoration.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-1, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.1 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-2, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.2 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-3, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.3 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-4, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.4 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-5, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.4 作山1号墳・5号墳出土 金属製品.JPG|Metal grave goods from Kofun No.1 and No.5 作山1号墳出土 合子形埴輪・丹後型円筒埴輪.JPG|Haniwa from Kofun No.1 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsukuriyama Kofun Group}} *[https://yosano-kankou.net/kankou/kofunpark/ Yosano Tourism home page] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster | native_name =作山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukuriyama Kofungun (Yosano) gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|30|21.82|N|135|06|16.96|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Yosano, Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|30|21.82|N|135|06|16.96|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster'''|作山古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Yosano neighborhood of the town of [[Yosano, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The group consists of five tumuli which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191122 |title=作山古墳群 |trans-title= Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster is located at the base of the [[Tango Peninsula]] in northern Kyoto Prefecture. It consists of a total of five tumuli: one {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, two {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofuns and two {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style square Kofuns. Although of different shapes, all are small to medium-sized tumuli and are roughly contemporary, dating from the late [[Kofun period]], or the first half of the 5th century. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1929 and 1989, during which time a large quantity of cylindrical and figurative (house shaped, equipment shaped) ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were discovered. In the keyhole-shaped tumulus, a granite sarcophagus was found in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the bones of an adult male. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, beads and other jewelry, iron swords, iron spearheads, and iron farming implements such as sickles. A total of 26 burials were discovered in and around the remaining tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were also excavated.
'''Kofun No.1''' - This is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 28 meters and height of four meters, in two tiers. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and ''haniwa''. The sarcophagus was made of granite and was located in a burial chamber with a vestibule. Grave goods included one bronze mirror, two stone swords, 12 iron swords, four spearheads and two iron axes and two iron sickles, as well as 500 glass beads. In additional 15 subordinate burials were found at the foot of the mound.
'''Kofun No.2''' - This is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 26 meters and height of 3.5 meters. ''Haniwa'' have been found on the surface, but no ''fukiishi''. The details of the burial chamber are unclear, as the tumulus has not been excavated. In addition, 10 subordinate burials are found on the west side of the mound.
'''Kofun No.3''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 17 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. ''Haniwa'' have been found on the surface, but no ''fukiishi''. The details of the burial chamber are unclear; however, excavations have found large pot which originated from the western end of the [[Seto Inland Sea]].
'''Kofun No.4''' - This is a small [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] with a total length of 29 meters.Both ''Haniwa'' and ''fukiishi'' been found on the surface. The details of the burial chamber are unclear.
'''Kofun No.5''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 13 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. The burial chamber contained a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin with a length of 6.4 meters.Numerous grave goods were found.
Currently, the site, together with the [[Ebisuyama Kofun]], is open to the public as the Yosano Municipal Kofun Park after the restoration.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-1, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.1 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-2, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.2 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-3, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.3 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-4, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.4 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-5, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.4 作山1号墳・5号墳出土 金属製品.JPG|Metal grave goods from Kofun No.1 and No.5 作山1号墳出土 合子形埴輪・丹後型円筒埴輪.JPG|Haniwa from Kofun No.1 </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsukuriyama Kofun Group}} *[https://yosano-kankou.net/kankou/kofunpark/ Yosano Tourism home page] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55533395]] Wikidata name: Tsukuriyama Kofun (Yosano) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster | native_name =作山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukuriyama Kofungun (Yosano) gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|30|21.82|N|135|06|16.96|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|30|21.82|N|135|06|16.96|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster'''|作山古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Yosano neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The group consists of five tumuli which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191122 |title=作山古墳群 |trans-title= Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster is located at the base of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] in northern Kyoto Prefecture. It consists of a total of five tumuli: one {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, two {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofuns and two {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style square Kofuns. Although of different shapes, all are small to medium-sized tumuli and are roughly contemporary, dating from the late [[Kofun period]], or the first half of the 5th century. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1929 and 1989, during which time a large quantity of cylindrical and figurative (house shaped, equipment shaped) ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were discovered. In the keyhole-shaped tumulus, a granite sarcophagus was found in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the bones of an adult male. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, beads and other jewelry, iron swords, iron spearheads, and iron farming implements such as sickles. A total of 26 burials were discovered in and around the remaining tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were also excavated.
'''Kofun No.1''' - This is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 28 meters and height of four meters, in two tiers. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and ''haniwa''. The sarcophagus was made of granite and was located in a burial chamber with a vestibule. Grave goods included one bronze mirror, two stone swords, 12 iron swords, four spearheads and two iron axes and two iron sickles, as well as 500 glass beads. In additional 15 subordinate burials were found at the foot of the mound.
'''Kofun No.2''' - This is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 26 meters and height of 3.5 meters. ''Haniwa'' have been found on the surface, but no ''fukiishi''. The details of the burial chamber are unclear, as the tumulus has not been excavated. In addition, 10 subordinate burials are found on the west side of the mound.
'''Kofun No.3''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 17 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. ''Haniwa'' have been found on the surface, but no ''fukiishi''. The details of the burial chamber are unclear; however, excavations have found large pot which originated from the western end of the [[Seto Inland Sea]].
'''Kofun No.4''' - This is a small [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] with a total length of 29 meters.Both ''Haniwa'' and ''fukiishi'' been found on the surface. The details of the burial chamber are unclear.
'''Kofun No.5''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 13 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. The burial chamber contained a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin with a length of 6.4 meters.Numerous grave goods were found.
Currently, the site, together with the [[Ebisuyama Kofun]], is open to the public as the Yosano Municipal Kofun Park after the restoration.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-1, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.1 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-2, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.2 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-3, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.3 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-4, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.4 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-5, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.4 作山1号墳・5号墳出土 金属製品.JPG|Metal grave goods from Kofun No.1 and No.5 作山1号墳出土 合子形埴輪・丹後型円筒埴輪.JPG|Haniwa from Kofun No.1 </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsukuriyama Kofun Group}} *[https://yosano-kankou.net/kankou/kofunpark/ Yosano Tourism home page] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tsukuriyama Kofun Group]][[en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Yosano)]][[ja:作山古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55533395]] Wikidata name: Tsukuriyama Kofun (Yosano) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster | native_name =作山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukuriyama Kofungun (Yosano) gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|30|21.82|N|135|06|16.96|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}}; {{Coord|35.50606111|N|135.10471111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Yosano|qid=Q1203259}}| region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|30|21.82|N|135|06|16.96|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster'''|作山古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Yosano neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The group consists of five tumuli which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191122 |title=作山古墳群 |trans-title= Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster is located at the base of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] in northern Kyoto Prefecture. It consists of a total of five tumuli: one {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, two {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofuns and two {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style square Kofuns. Although of different shapes, all are small to medium-sized tumuli and are roughly contemporary, dating from the late [[Kofun period]], or the first half of the 5th century. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1929 and 1989, during which time a large quantity of cylindrical and figurative (house shaped, equipment shaped) ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were discovered. In the keyhole-shaped tumulus, a granite sarcophagus was found in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the bones of an adult male. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, beads and other jewelry, iron swords, iron spearheads, and iron farming implements such as sickles. A total of 26 burials were discovered in and around the remaining tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were also excavated.
'''Kofun No.1''' - This is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 28 meters and height of four meters, in two tiers. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and ''haniwa''. The sarcophagus was made of granite and was located in a burial chamber with a vestibule. Grave goods included one bronze mirror, two stone swords, 12 iron swords, four spearheads and two iron axes and two iron sickles, as well as 500 glass beads. In additional 15 subordinate burials were found at the foot of the mound.
'''Kofun No.2''' - This is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 26 meters and height of 3.5 meters. ''Haniwa'' have been found on the surface, but no ''fukiishi''. The details of the burial chamber are unclear, as the tumulus has not been excavated. In addition, 10 subordinate burials are found on the west side of the mound.
'''Kofun No.3''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 17 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. ''Haniwa'' have been found on the surface, but no ''fukiishi''. The details of the burial chamber are unclear; however, excavations have found large pot which originated from the western end of the [[Seto Inland Sea]].
'''Kofun No.4''' - This is a small [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] with a total length of 29 meters.Both ''Haniwa'' and ''fukiishi'' been found on the surface. The details of the burial chamber are unclear.
'''Kofun No.5''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 13 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. The burial chamber contained a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin with a length of 6.4 meters.Numerous grave goods were found.
Currently, the site, together with the [[Ebisuyama Kofun]], is open to the public as the Yosano Municipal Kofun Park after the restoration.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-1, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.1 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-2, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.2 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-3, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.3 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-4, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.4 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-5, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.4 作山1号墳・5号墳出土 金属製品.JPG|Metal grave goods from Kofun No.1 and No.5 作山1号墳出土 合子形埴輪・丹後型円筒埴輪.JPG|Haniwa from Kofun No.1 </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Tsukuriyama Kofun Group}} *[https://yosano-kankou.net/kankou/kofunpark/ Yosano Tourism home page] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsukuriyama Kofun Group]][[en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Yosano)]][[ja:作山古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55533395]] Wikidata name: Tsukuriyama Kofun (Yosano) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster | native_name =作山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukuriyama Kofungun (Yosano) gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|30|21.82|N|135|06|16.96|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}}; {{Coord|35.50606111|N|135.10471111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Yosano|WD=Q1203259}}| region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|30|21.82|N|135|06|16.96|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster'''|作山古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Yosano neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The group consists of five tumuli which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191122 |title=作山古墳群 |trans-title= Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster is located at the base of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] in northern Kyoto Prefecture. It consists of a total of five tumuli: one {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, two {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofuns and two {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style square Kofuns. Although of different shapes, all are small to medium-sized tumuli and are roughly contemporary, dating from the late [[Kofun period]], or the first half of the 5th century. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1929 and 1989, during which time a large quantity of cylindrical and figurative (house shaped, equipment shaped) ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were discovered. In the keyhole-shaped tumulus, a granite sarcophagus was found in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the bones of an adult male. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, beads and other jewelry, iron swords, iron spearheads, and iron farming implements such as sickles. A total of 26 burials were discovered in and around the remaining tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were also excavated.
'''Kofun No.1''' - This is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 28 meters and height of four meters, in two tiers. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and ''haniwa''. The sarcophagus was made of granite and was located in a burial chamber with a vestibule. Grave goods included one bronze mirror, two stone swords, 12 iron swords, four spearheads and two iron axes and two iron sickles, as well as 500 glass beads. In additional 15 subordinate burials were found at the foot of the mound.
'''Kofun No.2''' - This is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 26 meters and height of 3.5 meters. ''Haniwa'' have been found on the surface, but no ''fukiishi''. The details of the burial chamber are unclear, as the tumulus has not been excavated. In addition, 10 subordinate burials are found on the west side of the mound.
'''Kofun No.3''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 17 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. ''Haniwa'' have been found on the surface, but no ''fukiishi''. The details of the burial chamber are unclear; however, excavations have found large pot which originated from the western end of the [[Seto Inland Sea]].
'''Kofun No.4''' - This is a small [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] with a total length of 29 meters.Both ''Haniwa'' and ''fukiishi'' been found on the surface. The details of the burial chamber are unclear.
'''Kofun No.5''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 13 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. The burial chamber contained a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin with a length of 6.4 meters.Numerous grave goods were found.
Currently, the site, together with the [[Ebisuyama Kofun]], is open to the public as the Yosano Municipal Kofun Park after the restoration.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-1, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.1 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-2, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.2 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-3, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.3 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-4, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.4 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-5, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.4 作山1号墳・5号墳出土 金属製品.JPG|Metal grave goods from Kofun No.1 and No.5 作山1号墳出土 合子形埴輪・丹後型円筒埴輪.JPG|Haniwa from Kofun No.1 </gallery>

'''Kofun No.5''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 13 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. The burial chamber contained a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin with a length of 6.4 meters.Numerous grave goods were found.
Currently, the site, together with the [[Ebisuyama Kofun]], is open to the public as the Yosano Municipal Kofun Park after the restoration.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsukuriyama Kofun Group]][[en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Yosano)]][[ja:作山古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55533395]] Wikidata name: Tsukuriyama Kofun (Yosano) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster | native_name =作山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukuriyama Kofungun (Yosano) gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|30|21.82|N|135|06|16.96|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}}; {{Coord|35.50606111|N|135.10471111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Yosano|WD=Q1203259}}| region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|30|21.82|N|135|06|16.96|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster'''|作山古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Yosano neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The group consists of five tumuli which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191122 |title=作山古墳群 |trans-title= Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster is located at the base of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] in northern Kyoto Prefecture. It consists of a total of five tumuli: one {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, two {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofuns and two {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style square Kofuns. Although of different shapes, all are small to medium-sized tumuli and are roughly contemporary, dating from the late [[Kofun period]], or the first half of the 5th century. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1929 and 1989, during which time a large quantity of cylindrical and figurative (house shaped, equipment shaped) ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were discovered. In the keyhole-shaped tumulus, a granite sarcophagus was found in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the bones of an adult male. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, beads and other jewelry, iron swords, iron spearheads, and iron farming implements such as sickles. A total of 26 burials were discovered in and around the remaining tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were also excavated.
'''Kofun No.1''' - This is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 28 meters and height of four meters, in two tiers. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and ''haniwa''. The sarcophagus was made of granite and was located in a burial chamber with a vestibule. Grave goods included one bronze mirror, two stone swords, 12 iron swords, four spearheads and two iron axes and two iron sickles, as well as 500 glass beads. In additional 15 subordinate burials were found at the foot of the mound.
'''Kofun No.2''' - This is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 26 meters and height of 3.5 meters. ''Haniwa'' have been found on the surface, but no ''fukiishi''. The details of the burial chamber are unclear, as the tumulus has not been excavated. In addition, 10 subordinate burials are found on the west side of the mound.
'''Kofun No.3''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 17 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. ''Haniwa'' have been found on the surface, but no ''fukiishi''. The details of the burial chamber are unclear; however, excavations have found large pot which originated from the western end of the [[Seto Inland Sea]].
'''Kofun No.4''' - This is a small [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] with a total length of 29 meters.Both ''Haniwa'' and ''fukiishi'' been found on the surface. The details of the burial chamber are unclear.
'''Kofun No.5''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 13 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. The burial chamber contained a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin with a length of 6.4 meters.Numerous grave goods were found.
Currently, the site, together with the [[Ebisuyama Kofun]], is open to the public as the Yosano Municipal Kofun Park after the restoration.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-1, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.1 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-2, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.2 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-3, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.3 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-4, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.4 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-5, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.4 作山1号墳・5号墳出土 金属製品.JPG|Metal grave goods from Kofun No.1 and No.5 作山1号墳出土 合子形埴輪・丹後型円筒埴輪.JPG|Haniwa from Kofun No.1 </gallery>

'''Kofun No.3''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 17 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. ''Haniwa'' have been found on the surface, but no ''fukiishi''. The details of the burial chamber are unclear; however, excavations have found large pot which originated from the western end of the [[Seto Inland Sea]].
'''Kofun No.4''' - This is a small [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] with a total length of 29 meters.Both ''Haniwa'' and ''fukiishi'' been found on the surface. The details of the burial chamber are unclear.
'''Kofun No.5''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 13 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. The burial chamber contained a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin with a length of 6.4 meters.Numerous grave goods were found.
Currently, the site, together with the [[Ebisuyama Kofun]], is open to the public as the Yosano Municipal Kofun Park after the restoration.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsukuriyama Kofun Group]][[en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Yosano)]][[ja:作山古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55533395]] Wikidata name: Tsukuriyama Kofun (Yosano) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster | native_name =作山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukuriyama Kofungun (Yosano) gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|30|21.82|N|135|06|16.96|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}}; {{Coord|35.50606111|N|135.10471111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Yosano|WD=Q1203259}}| region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|30|21.82|N|135|06|16.96|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster'''|作山古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Yosano neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The group consists of five tumuli which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191122 |title=作山古墳群 |trans-title= Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster is located at the base of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] in northern Kyoto Prefecture. It consists of a total of five tumuli: one {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, two {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofuns and two {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style square Kofuns. Although of different shapes, all are small to medium-sized tumuli and are roughly contemporary, dating from the late [[Kofun period]], or the first half of the 5th century. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1929 and 1989, during which time a large quantity of cylindrical and figurative (house shaped, equipment shaped) ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were discovered. In the keyhole-shaped tumulus, a granite sarcophagus was found in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the bones of an adult male. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, beads and other jewelry, iron swords, iron spearheads, and iron farming implements such as sickles. A total of 26 burials were discovered in and around the remaining tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were also excavated.
'''Kofun No.1''' - This is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 28 meters and height of four meters, in two tiers. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and ''haniwa''. The sarcophagus was made of granite and was located in a burial chamber with a vestibule. Grave goods included one bronze mirror, two stone swords, 12 iron swords, four spearheads and two iron axes and two iron sickles, as well as 500 glass beads. In additional 15 subordinate burials were found at the foot of the mound.
'''Kofun No.2''' - This is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 26 meters and height of 3.5 meters. ''Haniwa'' have been found on the surface, but no ''fukiishi''. The details of the burial chamber are unclear, as the tumulus has not been excavated. In addition, 10 subordinate burials are found on the west side of the mound.
'''Kofun No.3''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 17 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. ''Haniwa'' have been found on the surface, but no ''fukiishi''. The details of the burial chamber are unclear; however, excavations have found large pot which originated from the western end of the [[Seto Inland Sea]].
'''Kofun No.4''' - This is a small [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] with a total length of 29 meters.Both ''Haniwa'' and ''fukiishi'' been found on the surface. The details of the burial chamber are unclear.
'''Kofun No.5''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 13 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. The burial chamber contained a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin with a length of 6.4 meters.Numerous grave goods were found.
Currently, the site, together with the [[Ebisuyama Kofun]], is open to the public as the Yosano Municipal Kofun Park after the restoration.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-1, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.1 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-2, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.2 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-3, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.3 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-4, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.4 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-5, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.4 作山1号墳・5号墳出土 金属製品.JPG|Metal grave goods from Kofun No.1 and No.5 作山1号墳出土 合子形埴輪・丹後型円筒埴輪.JPG|Haniwa from Kofun No.1 </gallery>

The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and ''haniwa''. The sarcophagus was made of granite and was located in a burial chamber with a vestibule. Grave goods included one bronze mirror, two stone swords, 12 iron swords, four spearheads and two iron axes and two iron sickles, as well as 500 glass beads. In additional 15 subordinate burials were found at the foot of the mound.
'''Kofun No.2''' - This is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 26 meters and height of 3.5 meters. ''Haniwa'' have been found on the surface, but no ''fukiishi''. The details of the burial chamber are unclear, as the tumulus has not been excavated. In addition, 10 subordinate burials are found on the west side of the mound.
'''Kofun No.3''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 17 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. ''Haniwa'' have been found on the surface, but no ''fukiishi''. The details of the burial chamber are unclear; however, excavations have found large pot which originated from the western end of the [[Seto Inland Sea]].
'''Kofun No.4''' - This is a small [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] with a total length of 29 meters.Both ''Haniwa'' and ''fukiishi'' been found on the surface. The details of the burial chamber are unclear.
'''Kofun No.5''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 13 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. The burial chamber contained a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin with a length of 6.4 meters.Numerous grave goods were found.
Currently, the site, together with the [[Ebisuyama Kofun]], is open to the public as the Yosano Municipal Kofun Park after the restoration.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-1, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.1 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-2, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.2 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-3, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.3 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-4, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.4 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-5, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.4 作山1号墳・5号墳出土 金属製品.JPG|Metal grave goods from Kofun No.1 and No.5 作山1号墳出土 合子形埴輪・丹後型円筒埴輪.JPG|Haniwa from Kofun No.1 </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsukuriyama Kofun Group]][[en:Tsukuriyama Kofun (Yosano)]][[ja:作山古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55533395]] Wikidata name: Tsukuriyama Kofun (Yosano) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster | native_name =作山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsukuriyama Kofungun (Yosano) gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|30|21.82|N|135|06|16.96|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}}; {{Coord|35.50606111|N|135.10471111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Yosano|WD=Q1203259}}| region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|30|21.82|N|135|06|16.96|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster'''|作山古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Yosano neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The group consists of five tumuli which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191122 |title=作山古墳群 |trans-title= Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster is located at the base of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] in northern Kyoto Prefecture. It consists of a total of five tumuli: one {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, two {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofuns and two {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style square Kofuns. Although of different shapes, all are small to medium-sized tumuli and are roughly contemporary, dating from the late [[Kofun period]], or the first half of the 5th century. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1929 and 1989, during which time a large quantity of cylindrical and figurative (house shaped, equipment shaped) ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were discovered. In the keyhole-shaped tumulus, a granite sarcophagus was found in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the bones of an adult male. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, beads and other jewelry, iron swords, iron spearheads, and iron farming implements such as sickles. A total of 26 burials were discovered in and around the remaining tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were also excavated.
'''Kofun No.1''' - This is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 28 meters and height of four meters, in two tiers. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and ''haniwa''. The sarcophagus was made of granite and was located in a burial chamber with a vestibule. Grave goods included one bronze mirror, two stone swords, 12 iron swords, four spearheads and two iron axes and two iron sickles, as well as 500 glass beads. In additional 15 subordinate burials were found at the foot of the mound.
'''Kofun No.2''' - This is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 26 meters and height of 3.5 meters. ''Haniwa'' have been found on the surface, but no ''fukiishi''. The details of the burial chamber are unclear, as the tumulus has not been excavated. In addition, 10 subordinate burials are found on the west side of the mound.
'''Kofun No.3''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 17 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. ''Haniwa'' have been found on the surface, but no ''fukiishi''. The details of the burial chamber are unclear; however, excavations have found large pot which originated from the western end of the [[Seto Inland Sea]].
'''Kofun No.4''' - This is a small [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] with a total length of 29 meters.Both ''Haniwa'' and ''fukiishi'' been found on the surface. The details of the burial chamber are unclear.
'''Kofun No.5''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 13 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. The burial chamber contained a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin with a length of 6.4 meters.Numerous grave goods were found.
Currently, the site, together with the [[Ebisuyama Kofun]], is open to the public as the Yosano Municipal Kofun Park after the restoration.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-1, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.1 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-2, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.2 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-3, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.3 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-4, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.4 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-5, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.4 作山1号墳・5号墳出土 金属製品.JPG|Metal grave goods from Kofun No.1 and No.5 作山1号墳出土 合子形埴輪・丹後型円筒埴輪.JPG|Haniwa from Kofun No.1 </gallery>

The group consists of five tumuli which were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1930.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191122 |title=作山古墳群 |trans-title= Tsukuriyama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsukuriyama Kofun cluster is located at the base of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] in northern Kyoto Prefecture. It consists of a total of five tumuli: one {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, two {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofuns and two {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style square Kofuns. Although of different shapes, all are small to medium-sized tumuli and are roughly contemporary, dating from the late [[Kofun period]], or the first half of the 5th century. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1929 and 1989, during which time a large quantity of cylindrical and figurative (house shaped, equipment shaped) ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were discovered. In the keyhole-shaped tumulus, a granite sarcophagus was found in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the bones of an adult male. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, beads and other jewelry, iron swords, iron spearheads, and iron farming implements such as sickles. A total of 26 burials were discovered in and around the remaining tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were also excavated.
'''Kofun No.1''' - This is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 28 meters and height of four meters, in two tiers. The surface was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and ''haniwa''. The sarcophagus was made of granite and was located in a burial chamber with a vestibule. Grave goods included one bronze mirror, two stone swords, 12 iron swords, four spearheads and two iron axes and two iron sickles, as well as 500 glass beads. In additional 15 subordinate burials were found at the foot of the mound.
'''Kofun No.2''' - This is a {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a diameter of 26 meters and height of 3.5 meters. ''Haniwa'' have been found on the surface, but no ''fukiishi''. The details of the burial chamber are unclear, as the tumulus has not been excavated. In addition, 10 subordinate burials are found on the west side of the mound.
'''Kofun No.3''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 17 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. ''Haniwa'' have been found on the surface, but no ''fukiishi''. The details of the burial chamber are unclear; however, excavations have found large pot which originated from the western end of the [[Seto Inland Sea]].
'''Kofun No.4''' - This is a small [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] with a total length of 29 meters.Both ''Haniwa'' and ''fukiishi'' been found on the surface. The details of the burial chamber are unclear.
'''Kofun No.5''' - This is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 13 meters on each side and with a height of 2 meters. The burial chamber contained a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin with a length of 6.4 meters.Numerous grave goods were found.
Currently, the site, together with the [[Ebisuyama Kofun]], is open to the public as the Yosano Municipal Kofun Park after the restoration.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-1, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.1 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-2, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.2 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-3, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.3 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-4, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.4 Tsukuriyama Kofun Group-5, zenkei.jpg|Kofun No.4 作山1号墳・5号墳出土 金属製品.JPG|Metal grave goods from Kofun No.1 and No.5 作山1号墳出土 合子形埴輪・丹後型円筒埴輪.JPG|Haniwa from Kofun No.1 </gallery>
